headlineSummary: Anthropic trends positive on mixed but mostly favorable feedback, with commenters praising Claude’s usefulness and output [E1][E2][E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic is viewed positively overall: Claude is credited with speeding up real work and producing useful implementations, while one comment is mixed but still ends with a successful result [E1][E2][E3].

evidence.excerpt: This is so nicely presented it’s tempting me to have Claude whip up an implementation.

annotations.rationale: The commenter suggests Claude could readily produce an implementation, indicating mild confidence in its usefulness/capability.

evidence.excerpt: Most recently I got claude to rewrite my transaction rules engine https://djharper.dev/post/2025/08/19/using-llms-to-turn-scri... into something nicer with a UI. This would have taken days to build in the before times, and I probably would not have bothered because it's overkill for 1 user (me)

annotations.rationale: The commenter credits Claude with improving the tool and saving days of work, which is clear mild praise for performance/usefulness.

evidence.excerpt: I spent about a week insulting Claude to get port it to x86_64. It's running nicely in QEMU.

annotations.rationale: The wording mixes frustration ('insulting Claude') with a successful outcome ('It's running nicely in QEMU'), yielding a mixed overall stance.
